criterion: at least eight real layer-sublattice directions are scanned
interpretation: The first major-revision family scan is broader than one ansatz.

criterion: all scanned matrices are real, symmetric, and Hermitian
interpretation: The current candidate family stays within the intended real even-parity test set.

criterion: the original M1=taux_sigmax direction is included
interpretation: The family scan can be compared directly with the current manuscript result.

criterion: all fixed-Frobenius eta=1 rows are positive
interpretation: The positive normalized signature is not unique to the original direction at this grid.

criterion: minimum fixed-Frobenius response exceeds five percent in the first-pass family scan
interpretation: The family response has comparable scale to the manuscript's main diagnostic.
The normalization dependence remains central and cannot be hidden in the revision.

criterion: fixed-Delta0 absolute response is less than half the weakest fixed-Frobenius response
interpretation: The control remains substantially weaker than the normalized family response.
